questions.text: Which of the following is the most recommended legal entity for an Indian Web3 startup aiming to raise funds and limit personal liability?

options.text: Sole Proprietorship
Private Limited Company
Unregistered Partnership
Personal bank account

questions.explanation: A Private Limited Company offers limited liability, allows equity structuring (ESOPs, investment rounds), and is acceptable for MST grants. Sole proprietorships expose personal assets. Unregistered partnerships lack legal clarity.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct Answer: B - Explanation

A Private Limited Company offers limited liability, allows equity structuring (ESOPs, investment rounds), and is acceptable for MST grants. Sole proprietorships expose personal assets. Unregistered partnerships lack legal clarity.

questions.text: What is the primary purpose of a Founder IP Assignment Agreement?

options.text: To register the company with the ROC
To legally transfer all code and design assets from individual founders to the company
To define the token utility
To open a bank account for the startup

questions.explanation: Without an IP assignment, the code remains the personal property of the founder who wrote it. If that founder leaves, the company loses the right to use the code - a fatal blow.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct Answer: B - Explanation

Without an IP assignment, the code remains the personal property of the founder who wrote it. If that founder leaves, the company loses the right to use the code - a fatal blow.

questions.text: In tokenomics, what is a "vesting cliff"?

options.text: The total maximum supply of a token
A period (usually 1 year) before any team tokens begin to unlock
The moment a token is listed on a DEX
A mechanism to burn tokens

questions.explanation: A vesting cliff means no tokens are released until a specified time (commonly 12 months). After the cliff, tokens unlock gradually (monthly) over the remaining vesting period.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct Answer: B - Explanation

A vesting cliff means no tokens are released until a specified time (commonly 12 months). After the cliff, tokens unlock gradually (monthly) over the remaining vesting period.

questions.text: Which of the following is considered a "sink" mechanism in tokenomics?

options.text: Minting new tokens for liquidity providers
Using a portion of protocol fees to buy back and burn tokens
Airdropping tokens to early users
Increasing the total supply through inflation

questions.explanation: A sink removes tokens from circulation, creating deflationary pressure. Buy-back-and-burn is a classic sink. Minting and airdrops increase supply - they are sources, not sinks.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct Answer: B - Explanation

A sink removes tokens from circulation, creating deflationary pressure. Buy-back-and-burn is a classic sink. Minting and airdrops increase supply - they are sources, not sinks.

questions.text: According to the "Golden Triangle" for Web3 founding teams, which three roles are considered essential?

options.text: 3 Solidity developers, 1 designer, 1 community manager
Visionary (Product), Builder (Tech), Marketer (Business)
CEO, CFO, COO
Smart contract auditor, frontend dev, token economist

questions.explanation: While deep technical skills are crucial, without someone to define the product vision and someone to drive user adoption, a startup cannot succeed. The three roles must be complementary.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct Answer: B - Explanation

While deep technical skills are crucial, without someone to define the product vision and someone to drive user adoption, a startup cannot succeed. The three roles must be complementary.

questions.text: Why do Web3 investors and grant programs (like MST) specifically look for a "vesting schedule" in the team allocation?

options.text: To ensure the token price pumps on day one
To align long-term incentives and prevent immediate dumping by founders
To increase the total supply slowly
To comply with Indian labor laws

questions.explanation: Vesting ensures founders stay committed to the project for years, not days. Without it, a founder could sell all their tokens immediately after launch and abandon the protocol.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct Answer: B - Explanation

Vesting ensures founders stay committed to the project for years, not days. Without it, a founder could sell all their tokens immediately after launch and abandon the protocol.

questions.text: "A solo founder with no co-founders is generally viewed as a strong indicator of success by Web3 venture capitalists."
Using your own capstone project (or a hypothetical MST-based dApp), complete the following:
questions.explanation:
2 marks - Legal: Correct entity choice (Pvt Ltd / DAO) with sound justification. Mention of IP assignment.
                3 marks - Tokenomics: Clear supply, allocation percentages, defined utility, vesting schedule, and at least one sink mechanism.
                2 marks - Team: Identification of the three roles with names/skills and a justified equity split (with vesting).
                1 mark - Checklist: Completed 20-point checklist submitted as PDF.
questions.modelAnswer:
📊 Marks Breakdown

2 marks - Legal: Correct entity choice (Pvt Ltd / DAO) with sound justification. Mention of IP assignment.
3 marks - Tokenomics: Clear supply, allocation percentages, defined utility, vesting schedule, and at least one sink mechanism.
2 marks - Team: Identification of the three roles with names/skills and a justified equity split (with vesting).
1 mark - Checklist: Completed 20-point checklist submitted as PDF.

Note: The total answer should be approximately 800-1000 words. Use bullet points for allocation breakdown.
